text.title: Context Caching
text.summary: Gemini context caching stores large documents server-side for reuse across queries, cutting cost by 75%; requires minimum 32K tokens and explicit TTL management.

rule.statement: Check token count before creating a cache — creating a cache on content below 32K tokens raises an API error.
rule.rationale: The 32K minimum is a hard API constraint; attempting to cache small documents wastes a round-trip and surfaces an uninformative error.

example.code:
from google.generativeai import caching import google.generativeai as genai
document = genai.upload_file("large_document.pdf")
cache = caching.CachedContent.create( model="gemini-1.5-pro", display_name="Large document cache", system_instruction="You are an expert document analyzer.", contents=[document], ttl="3600s" # 1 hour ) print(f"Cache created: {cache.name}, tokens: {cache.usage_metadata.total_token_count}")

model = genai.GenerativeModel.from_cached_content(cache)
# All queries reuse cached document tokens — 75% cheaper than full-price input response = model.generate_content("Summarize the main points") response2 = model.generate_content("What are the key dates mentioned?")
example.note: Cost: $0.01875/M tokens cached vs $0.075/M tokens uncached (gemini-1.5-pro). Break-even at 2+ queries.

rule.statement: Set TTL to match query pattern: 1 hour for interactive sessions, 24 hours for batch jobs. Extend before expiry, not after.
rule.rationale: Expired caches require re-creation including re-uploading the document; extending TTL is a cheaper operation than re-upload.

example.code:
# List all caches for c in caching.CachedContent.list(): print(f"{c.name}: {c.display_name}")
# Extend TTL cache.update(ttl="7200s")
# Delete when done cache.delete()
